project `hdf5`. This is an agentic, first-principles judgment — there is no single metric. The
question: can a fuzzer driven by this harness thoroughly exercise this attack surface?

Attack surface: Property list encode/decode
APIs: H5Pencode2, H5Pdecode, H5Pget_filter2, H5Pclose
Harness binary: `h5_plist_encode_decode_fuzzer`

Method (use your tools):
1. Read the harness source under `/work/harness-proj/fuzz-proj` — does it drive these APIs the way a real caller
   would, feeding fuzzer bytes into the meaningful inputs (not hardcoding or short-circuiting them)?
2. Read the relevant target source under `/src` — what input space / branches should a
   thorough harness reach? Is a major part of the surface left unreachable as written?

   features=2848, corpus=58. Did coverage grow then plateau in a
   way consistent with the surface being explored, or does it look stuck/shallow?

Decide: sufficient=true if a fuzzer driven by this harness can thoroughly explore this attack surface.
Otherwise sufficient=false with concrete `feedback` on what to change in the harness (e.g. drive an uncovered
entry point, vary a mode/flag, stop discarding part of the input). Return ONLY the JSON verdict.

12	
13	/*
14	 * Fuzzing harness for the HDF5 property-list encode/decode surface.
15	 *
16	 * Attack surface: H5Pdecode / H5Pencode2 / H5Pget_filter2 / H5Pclose
17	 *
18	 * Strategy:
19	 *  1. Feed raw fuzzer bytes directly to H5Pdecode() – this is the primary
20	 *     parser target.  A valid encoded plist starts with a recognisable
21	 *     header; invalid blobs should be rejected cleanly, not crash.
22	 *  2. On success, exercise the decoded plist:
23	 *     a. Re-encode with H5Pencode2() (size query, then fill).
24	 *     b. For plists that carry a filter pipeline (DCPL / GCPL / OCPL),
25	 *        iterate every filter with H5Pget_filter2().
26	 *  3. Always release with H5Pclose().
27	 *
28	 * The harness never violates API contracts:
29	 *  - cd_nelmts is initialised before each H5Pget_filter2 call (required).
30	 *  - name buffer is always namelen bytes large.
31	 *  - We only call H5Pget_filter2 when H5Pget_nfilters() returns > 0.
32	 *  - H5Pencode2 is called with H5P_DEFAULT as fapl_id (documented as valid).
33	 */
34	
35	#include "hdf5.h"
36	
37	#include <stdint.h>
38	#include <stdlib.h>
39	#include <string.h>
40	
41	/* Maximum number of filter cd_values we will allocate per filter. */
42	#define MAX_CD_NELMTS 64
43	/* Name buffer length passed to H5Pget_filter2. */
44	#define FILTER_NAME_LEN 256
45	
46	int LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput(const uint8_t *data, size_t size)
47	{
48	    /* Silence HDF5 error output – we expect lots of malformed input. */
49	    H5Eset_auto2(H5E_DEFAULT, NULL, NULL);
50	
51	    if (size == 0)
52	        return 0;
53	
54	    /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
55	    /* 1.  Attempt to decode the fuzzer-supplied blob as a property list.  */
56	    /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
57	    hid_t plist_id = H5Pdecode(data);
58	    if (plist_id == H5I_INVALID_HID)
59	        return 0; /* malformed input – expected, not a bug */
60	
61	    /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
62	    /* 2a. Re-encode: first query the required buffer size.                */
63	    /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
64	    size_t enc_size = 0;
65	    if (H5Pencode2(plist_id, NULL, &enc_size, H5P_DEFAULT) >= 0 && enc_size > 0) {
66	        void *enc_buf = malloc(enc_size);
67	        if (enc_buf) {
68	            /* Fill the buffer with the encoded plist. */
69	            (void)H5Pencode2(plist_id, enc_buf, &enc_size, H5P_DEFAULT);
70	            free(enc_buf);
71	        }
72	    }
73	
74	    /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
75	    /* 2b. Query filter pipeline (only valid for OCPL subclasses).         */
76	    /*     H5Pget_nfilters returns a negative value for non-filter plists  */
77	    /*     so we guard on > 0.                                             */
78	    /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
79	    int nfilters = H5Pget_nfilters(plist_id);
80	    for (int i = 0; i < nfilters; i++) {
81	        unsigned int  flags       = 0;
82	        size_t        cd_nelmts   = MAX_CD_NELMTS; /* in/out: must be set by caller */
83	        unsigned      cd_values[MAX_CD_NELMTS];
84	        char          name[FILTER_NAME_LEN];
85	        unsigned int  filter_config = 0;
86	
87	        memset(cd_values, 0, sizeof(cd_values));
88	        memset(name, 0, sizeof(name));
89	
90	        H5Z_filter_t fid = H5Pget_filter2(plist_id,
91	                                           (unsigned)i,
92	                                           &flags,
93	                                           &cd_nelmts,
94	                                           cd_values,
95	                                           FILTER_NAME_LEN,
96	                                           name,
97	                                           &filter_config);
98	        (void)fid; /* result used only to drive coverage */
99	    }
100	
101	    /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
102	    /* 3.  Release resources.                                               */
103	    /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
104	    H5Pclose(plist_id);
105	
106	    return 0;
107	}
108

content.content:
700	    Returns non-negative ID of new property list object on success, negative
701	        on failure.
702	 DESCRIPTION
703	     Decodes a property list from a binary buffer. The contents of the buffer
704	     contain the values for the corresponding properties of the plist. The decode
705	     callback of a certain property decodes its value from the buffer and sets it
706	     in the property list.
707	 GLOBAL VARIABLES
708	 COMMENTS, BUGS, ASSUMPTIONS
709	     Properties in the property list that are not encoded in the serialized
710	     form retain their default value.
711	 EXAMPLES
712	 REVISION LOG
713	--------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
714	hid_t
715	H5P__decode(const void *buf)
716	{
717	    H5P_genplist_t  *plist;                            /* Property list to decode into */
718	    void            *value_buf = NULL;                 /* Pointer to buffer to use when decoding values */
719	    const uint8_t   *p         = (const uint8_t *)buf; /* Current pointer into buffer */
720	    H5P_plist_type_t type;                             /* Type of encoded property list */
721	    hid_t            plist_id       = -1;              /* ID of new property list */
722	    size_t           value_buf_size = 0;               /* Size of current value buffer */
723	    uint8_t          vers;                             /* Version of encoded property list */
724	    hid_t            ret_value = H5I_INVALID_HID;      /* Return value */
725	
726	    FUNC_ENTER_PACKAGE
727	
728	    /* Sanity check */
729	    if (NULL == p)
730	        HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_BADVALUE, FAIL, "decode buffer is NULL");
731	
732	    /* Get the version number of the encoded property list */
733	    vers = (uint8_t)*p++;
734	    if ((uint8_t)H5P_ENCODE_VERS != vers)
735	        HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_VERSION, FAIL, "bad version # of encoded information, expected %u, got %u",
736	                    (unsigned)H5P_ENCODE_VERS, (unsigned)vers);
737	
738	    /* Get the type of the property list */
739	    type = (H5P_plist_type_t)*p++;
740	    if (type <= H5P_TYPE_USER || type >= H5P_TYPE_MAX_TYPE)
741	        HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_BADRANGE, FAIL, "bad type of encoded information: %u", (unsigned)type);
742	
743	    /* Create new property list of the specified type */
744	    if ((plist_id = H5P__new_plist_of_type(type)) < 0)
745	        HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_VERSION, FAIL, "can't create property list of type: %u\n", (unsigned)type);
746	
747	    /* Get the property list object */
748	    if (NULL == (plist = (H5P_genplist_t *)H5I_object(plist_id)))
749	        HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_BADTYPE, FAIL, "not a property class");
750	
751	    /* Loop over encoded properties, deserializing their values */
752	    while (p) {
753	        H5P_genprop_t *prop; /* Pointer to property with same name */
754	        const char    *name; /* Pointer to property list name */
755	
756	        /* Check for end of serialized list of properties */
757	        if (0 == *p)
758	            break;
759	
760	        /* Get property list name */
761	        name = (const char *)p;
762	        p += strlen(name) + 1;
763	
764	        /* Find property with name */
765	        if (NULL == (prop = H5P__find_prop_plist(plist, name)))
766	            HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_NOTFOUND, FAIL, "property doesn't exist: '%s'", name);
767	
768	        /* Check if we should increase the size of the value buffer */
769	        if (prop->size > value_buf_size) {
770	            if (NULL == (value_buf = H5MM_realloc(value_buf, prop->size)))
771	                HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_CANTALLOC, FAIL, "decoding buffer allocation failed");
772	            value_buf_size = prop->size;
773	        } /* end if */
774	
775	        /* Decode serialized value */
776	        if (prop->decode) {
777	            /* Prepare & restore library for user callback */
778	            H5_BEFORE_USER_CB(FAIL)
779	                {
780	                    ret_value = (prop->decode)((const void **)&p, value_buf);
781	                }
782	            H5_AFTER_USER_CB(FAIL)
783	            if (ret_value < 0)
784	                HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_CANTDECODE, FAIL,
785	                            "property decoding routine failed, property: '%s'", name);
786	        } /* end if */
787	        else
788	            HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_NOTFOUND, FAIL, "no decode callback for property: '%s'", name);
789	
790	        /* Set the value for the property */
791	        if (H5P_poke(plist, name, value_buf) < 0)
792	            HGOTO_ERROR(H5E_PLIST, H5E_CANTSET, FAIL, "unable to set value for property: '%s'", name);
793	    } /* end while */
794	
795	    /* Set return value */
796	    ret_value = plist_id;
797	
798	done:
799	    /* Release resources */
